== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8490005004931 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町小蓮1189', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (南国市)]] {{wikidata link|Q18991294}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Koren 1189 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|54.43|N|133|36|40.61|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 7|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}It is also said that the original festival days were July 9 and October 7 {{Harv|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Koren, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. === History === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Tradition says it began when the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}} that settled this area worshiped their ancestral deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. However, there is also a theory that the shrine name “Ono” comes from a place name rather than the clan name{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Around the shrine are many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}, including {{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Ono Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, Ono Castle was built on the hill at the current site. It is said that when {{ill|lt=Kagawa Chikakazu|Q3676181|WD=Q3676181|1=Kagawa Chikakazu}}, second son of {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, returned after the decline of the Kagawa family, he was given that old Ono Castle{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Medieval and early modern documents such as ''Nanroshi'' also contain entries calling it “Toyooka Daimyōjin,” apparently confusing it with Toyooka Daimyōjin enshrined in the precincts{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine * Sabae Shrine === Historic sites === * Site of the former residence of {{ill|lt=Hirai Masazane|Q100454847|WD=Q100454847|lt=Hirai Zennojō Masazane|1=Hirai Masami}} - on the left side a short way up the shrine approach{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nankoku.lg.jp/download/?t=KHN&id=154&fid=34311|title=''Nankoku Historical Walk'' No. 58|publisher=Public Relations (Nankoku City, Kōchi)|date=2017-11-01|accessdate=2020-10-01}} *{{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ono Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Ono Shrine|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nangoku]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8490005004931 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町小蓮1189', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (南国市)]] {{wikidata link|Q18991294}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Koren 1189 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|54.43|N|133|36|40.61|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 7|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}It is also said that the original festival days were July 9 and October 7 {{Harv|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Koren, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. === History === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Tradition says it began when the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}} that settled this area worshiped their ancestral deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. However, there is also a theory that the shrine name “Ono” comes from a place name rather than the clan name{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Around the shrine are many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}, including {{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Ono Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, Ono Castle was built on the hill at the current site. It is said that when {{ill|lt=Kagawa Chikakazu|Q3676181|WD=Q3676181|1=Kagawa Chikakazu}}, second son of {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, returned after the decline of the Kagawa family, he was given that old Ono Castle{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Medieval and early modern documents such as ''Nanroshi'' also contain entries calling it “Toyooka Daimyōjin,” apparently confusing it with Toyooka Daimyōjin enshrined in the precincts{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine * Sabae Shrine === Historic sites === * Site of the former residence of {{ill|lt=Hirai Masazane|Q100454847|WD=Q100454847|lt=Hirai Zennojō Masazane|1=Hirai Masami}} - on the left side a short way up the shrine approach{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nankoku.lg.jp/download/?t=KHN&id=154&fid=34311|title=''Nankoku Historical Walk'' No. 58|publisher=Public Relations (Nankoku City, Kōchi)|date=2017-11-01|accessdate=2020-10-01}} *{{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ono Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Ono Shrine|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nangoku]] [[Category:南国市の建築物]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8490005004931 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町小蓮1189', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (南国市)]] {{wikidata link|Q18991294}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Koren 1189 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|54.43|N|133|36|40.61|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 7|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}It is also said that the original festival days were July 9 and October 7 {{Harv|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Koren, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. === History === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Tradition says it began when the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}} that settled this area worshiped their ancestral deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. However, there is also a theory that the shrine name “Ono” comes from a place name rather than the clan name{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Around the shrine are many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}, including {{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Ono Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, Ono Castle was built on the hill at the current site. It is said that when {{ill|lt=Kagawa Chikakazu|Q3676181|WD=Q3676181|1=Kagawa Chikakazu}}, second son of {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, returned after the decline of the Kagawa family, he was given that old Ono Castle{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Medieval and early modern documents such as ''Nanroshi'' also contain entries calling it “Toyooka Daimyōjin,” apparently confusing it with Toyooka Daimyōjin enshrined in the precincts{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine * Sabae Shrine === Historic sites === * Site of the former residence of {{ill|lt=Hirai Masazane|Q100454847|WD=Q100454847|lt=Hirai Zennojō Masazane|1=Hirai Masami}} - on the left side a short way up the shrine approach{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nankoku.lg.jp/download/?t=KHN&id=154&fid=34311|title=''Nankoku Historical Walk'' No. 58|publisher=Public Relations (Nankoku City, Kōchi)|date=2017-11-01|accessdate=2020-10-01}} *{{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ono Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Ono Shrine|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nangoku]] [[Category:Buildings in Nankoku]]

== Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 8490005004931 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '高知県南国市岡豊町小蓮1189', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:小野神社 (南国市)]] {{wikidata link|Q18991294}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) shaden.JPG|250px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}} Okō-chō Koren 1189 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|35|54.43|N|133|36|40.61|E|region:JP-39_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Ono Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建 = Unknown |本殿 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=August 7|Q2781|WD=Q2781|1=August 7}}It is also said that the original festival days were July 9 and October 7 {{Harv|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Kochi|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[File:Ono-jinja (Nankoku) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Koren, Okō-chō, {{ill|lt=Nankoku|Q861621|WD=Q861621|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|Q134093|WD=Q134093|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deity === The shrine enshrines the following one deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. * {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}} - ancestral deity of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. === History === The founding date is unknown{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Tradition says it began when the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}} that settled this area worshiped their ancestral deity{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. However, there is also a theory that the shrine name “Ono” comes from a place name rather than the clan name{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Around the shrine are many {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|WD=Q1141225|1=Kofun}}, including {{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'' compiled in {{ill|lt=927|Q30470|WD=Q30470|1=927}} ({{ill|lt=Enchō|Q1327274|WD=Q1327274|1=Enchō}} 5), the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'' lists “Ono Shrine” in {{ill|lt=Nagaoka District|Q1132123|WD=Q1132123|1=Nagaoka District, Kōchi}}, {{ill|lt=Tosa Province|Q857330|WD=Q857330|1=Tosa Province}}, and this is identified with this shrine{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In the {{ill|lt=medieval period|Q12554|WD=Q12554|1=Middle Ages}}, Ono Castle was built on the hill at the current site. It is said that when {{ill|lt=Kagawa Chikakazu|Q3676181|WD=Q3676181|1=Kagawa Chikakazu}}, second son of {{ill|lt=Chōsokabe Motochika|Q1070082|WD=Q1070082|1=Chōsokabe Motochika}}, returned after the decline of the Kagawa family, he was given that old Ono Castle{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. Medieval and early modern documents such as ''Nanroshi'' also contain entries calling it “Toyooka Daimyōjin,” apparently confusing it with Toyooka Daimyōjin enshrined in the precincts{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} under the {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}{{Sfn|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}. === Auxiliary shrines === * Wakamiya Shrine * Sabae Shrine === Historic sites === * Site of the former residence of {{ill|lt=Hirai Masazane|Q100454847|WD=Q100454847|lt=Hirai Zennojō Masazane|1=Hirai Masami}} - on the left side a short way up the shrine approach{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.city.nankoku.lg.jp/download/?t=KHN&id=154&fid=34311|title=''Nankoku Historical Walk'' No. 58|publisher=Public Relations (Nankoku City, Kōchi)|date=2017-11-01|accessdate=2020-10-01}} *{{ill|lt=Koren Kofun|Q20044371|WD=Q20044371|1=Koren Kofun}} === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=Ono Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 40 Place Names of Kōchi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=978-4582490404|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=Shikinaisha Research Society|author=Yamamoto Hiroshi|authorlink=山本大|chapter=Ono Shrine|year=1987|title=Survey Report on Shikinaisha Vol. 9|publisher={{ill|lt=Kogakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|Ono Shrine (Shikinaisha)|1987}}}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Nankoku)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Tosa Province]] [[Category:高知県の旧村社]] [[Category:Ono clan]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nangoku]] [[Category:Buildings in Nankoku]]
